subject: Public Health
title: Disease Surveillance Systems
description: A fast breakdown of Disease Surveillance Systems for builders.

hook.text: Before you can fight an outbreak, you need to know it's happening. That's surveillance.

buildup.text: Hospitals, labs, and clinics report cases to national health agencies in near real-time.

discovery.text: The WHO's Global Outbreak Alert system monitors disease signals from 196 countries simultaneously.

twist.text: Digital surveillance now scans social media and news to detect outbreaks before official reports.

climax.text: Early detection can shrink an epidemic's peak by weeks—saving thousands of lives.

punchline.text: You can't fight what you can't see coming.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of disease surveillance?
options: Detecting outbreaks early to enable rapid response
